description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, exposing a lighter stone color in contrast to the darker, solid portions of the block. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, with a solid, monolithic appearance. The block is relatively low in height compared to its width and length, giving it a squat, platform-like profile. The overall finish is matte, with a hard, durable surface typical of stone used in urban environments. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering, suggesting it is either new or well-maintained.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, creating a striped pattern of light and dark stone. The sides are smooth and solid, giving it a robust, platform-like appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the striped top surface with parallel grooves and a solid, smooth front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the block's width more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the striped top and a solid, unmarked rear face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the block's length and groove pattern.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely used as a standalone barrier or seating block
